reasoning: Confirmed real. At class-cei-core.php line 44, can_import() returns apply_filters('cei_allow_unsafe_imports', current_user_can('install_plugins')). This filter is the sole server-side capability guard called inside _import() at line 246 before processing a file upload. The outer guard in init() (line 58) only requires edit_theme_options, a lower capability. If any plugin/theme hooks 'cei_allow_unsafe_imports' and returns true, any user with edit_theme_options can bypass the install_plugins requirement and trigger the import path. The import operation calls wp_handle_upload(), unserialize() (with allowed_classes=false), writes theme mods, and can trigger SSRF via download_url() when importing images. The nonce at line 251 is embedded in the import form (control.php line 24), which is also gated on can_import() -- so a bypassed filter also reveals the nonce to the user, making the nonce check ineffective as a compensating control against this specific bypass. Severity is medium (not high/critical) because exploitation requires a second component (plugin, theme, or code) that hooks the filter carelessly or maliciously -- it is not directly exploitable by an external attacker without that prerequisite.
